Counsel for defendants presented the Court with a copy of the proof of service and the Court found the proof of service invalid. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANTS CORY BIRNBERG, CORY A. BIRNBERG, APC, BIRNBERG & ASSOCIATES' Motion To Strike Certain Portions Of Plaintiffs' Prayers For Relief In Their Third Complaint For Damages is denied for invalid proof of service. Proof of service does not state who served documents, being worded in the third person. (301/PJB) Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR # 8189

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Pltf'S Responses To Deft'S Discovery; Request For Monetary Sanctions; Memorandum; Declaration
Off Calendar Jun-15-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: NO APPEARANCES. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Defendants Cory Birnberg's Motion To Compel Plaintiffs' Responses To Defendant's Discovery is granted. No opposition has been filed. Plaintiffs' failure to respond was a misuse of the discovery process. Plaintiffs are ordered to respond within 15 days of notice of this order. Sanctions are awarded against Plaintiffs in the amount of $550, to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT
